sourceShortSummaries: At the Leviathan Inn, Rayna tells the party that the ocean's song has returned and begs them to recover a trapped fragment of it from a hidden shrine near Hamri.
The party questions Rayna, consults Yaz and Ovina, and learns that the vision likely points to a long-buried shrine of Guzo in the sea caves of Hamri's North Cliff.
sourceLongSummaries: The session opens with a broad recap of Hamri's recent history before Wazir gathers Ryu, Trok, Nerissa, and Ander at the Leviathan Inn to hear out a storm-battered sailor named Rayna. She explains that she has heard the ocean speak in visions since childhood, that the songs fell silent years ago after a nightmare of tearing and loss, and that only recently they returned with a plea about something trapped under rock yet still resting on the waves. The voice showed her a shrine of Guzo the Mariner and begged for a fragment of song to be restored, so she has come to Hamri asking for help from people she has been told can be trusted.

Still at the Leviathan, the group presses Rayna for more detail and works from Trok's sketch of the place shown in her vision. Yaz recognizes the picture as matching an old shrine of Guzo the Mariner once built in a sea cave on the North Cliff, while Ovina, an old dwarf who has been in Hamri for more than a century, remembers the cave and confirms it collapsed long ago near the last tower in the line. With their clues aligned around a buried coastal shrine and a likely low-tide approach, the party settles on taking Ryu's boat out in the morning to search the site directly.

sourceEntries.title: Recap and Rayna's Plea
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The opening stays in one continuous tavern setup scene: the DM recaps Hamri's recent history, brings the party together at the Leviathan, and has Rayna explain the dreams, the song fragment, and the hidden cave she wants found.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At the Leviathan Inn, Rayna tells the party that the ocean's song has returned and begs them to recover a trapped fragment of it from a hidden shrine near Hamri.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The session opens with a broad recap of Hamri's recent history before Wazir gathers Ryu, Trok, Nerissa, and Ander at the Leviathan Inn to hear out a storm-battered sailor named Rayna. She explains that she has heard the ocean speak in visions since childhood, that the songs fell silent years ago after a nightmare of tearing and loss, and that only recently they returned with a plea about something trapped under rock yet still resting on the waves. The voice showed her a shrine of Guzo the Mariner and begged for a fragment of song to be restored, so she has come to Hamri asking for help from people she has been told can be trusted.

locationRefs: North Cliff sea cave outside Hamri
buried sea cave shrine of Guzo
Guzo shrine inner temple and sea cave beach
sourceShortSummaries: The next morning the party sails to the North Cliff, finds the old collapse, and confirms that a blasted opening now leads back into the buried shrine cave.
Swimming through the breach, the party finds the blue-lit beach and ruined outer temple of Guzo's shrine, spots idle water creatures, and advances toward the building.
Inside the shrine, the party uncovers books and offerings, finds the ancestor prayer that opens a hidden stone passage, and reveals a trapped water elemental clutching the crystal fragment.

The trapped elemental offers to spare the party if they free it and let it take the crystal to Gazankoa, but the blue light's binding effect and the creature's threats convince the group to fight instead.
The elemental opens the fight with a brutal jet of water, hurling the party back, dropping Geo, and turning the doorway ambush into a hard scramble for control.
The shrine's blue light begins to work against the elemental, and Nerissa's magic drives it back while the lesser creatures crowd the stairs and start grappling the front line.
The wounded elemental turns to mist, slips through the wall, punishes fleeing minions, and is finally destroyed before the party cuts down the last shrine creatures.
After the fight, Nerissa hears the recovered crystal's grateful song, the party returns it to Rayna, and Rayna accepts the task of carrying it to Omni.
sourceLongSummaries: After sleeping on the plan, the group meets the next morning, takes breakfast, and boards Ryu's small boat with Rayna along for the ride. They follow the North Cliff shoreline to the place Ovina described, use scouting and shared checks to study the rockfall, and realize that the cave has recently been punched open again by some force far cleaner than a natural collapse. With Ryu linked through Turk's senses and Wazir's mechanical fish scouting alongside, they confirm there is still a navigable way into the old shrine beyond the rubble.

The party dives through the newly blasted underwater opening and surfaces inside a hidden sea cave whose interior matches Rayna's vision: a warm blue glow, a strip of sandy beach, and a white shrine building on the shore. They quickly realize the collapse was not the only recent disturbance, because the breach and the shattered doorway deeper inside both look as if magic blasted straight through stone and timber. A cluster of watery, frog-like shrine creatures loiters around the beach and temple, and after sizing up the scene the party presses inward instead of retreating for a longer plan.

With the outer approach secured for the moment, the party searches the main temple and the side chapel in earnest. They study the statue of Guzo, the glowing altar, scattered offerings, and a damaged stone chest that still preserves a prayer book, a saintly story collection, and a ledger of prayers left at the shrine. The Mawarin inscription about kneeling and praying Guzo's ancestor prayer leads Nerissa to the right text, and when she sings it the hidden slab door vanishes to expose a deeper chamber. Inside waits a large water elemental figure pinned by the shrine's blue light and holding the crystal fragment the party came to recover.

Before steel is drawn, the party talks with the elemental in the hidden chamber and learns how badly things have gone inside the shrine. The creature explains that it forced its way in, seized the crystal fragment, and triggered the blue light bound to the altar, which now prevents its teleportation and keeps it trapped despite the opened passage. It offers to return to the plane of water and even let the party present the crystal to Gazankoa, but the more it talks the clearer it becomes that it will simply keep hunting the shard and anyone carrying it. By the end of the exchange, the party settles on bottling the creature up inside the shrine and killing it rather than trusting any bargain.

When the parley breaks down, initiative starts and the trapped elemental wastes no time, lashing the shrine interior with a massive jet of frigid water. The opening blast batters Trok, Ryu, Ander, Geo, and the nearby shrine creatures, immediately showing how dangerous the foe is even while confined by the blue light. Ryu recovers enough to answer with thrown trident strikes and Hunter's Mark, Ander fights his way back into the doorway, and the party's neat plan to contain the creature turns into a messy opening brawl in the temple.

As the melee settles into its middle phase, the same blue glow binding the elemental starts actively helping the party. Nerissa's dissonant whispers bite especially hard under the altar's influence and force the elemental backward into the chamber, where opportunity attacks from Trok and Wazir leave it staggering and badly wounded. The lesser shrine creatures rush the stairs and doorway, hardening their bodies and then grappling Ryu and Ander with watery scimitars, but the party keeps pressure on the main foe until it is below a quarter of its strength and ready to change tactics.

The final phase begins when the badly wounded elemental abandons a straight fight and turns into watery mist to move away from the party. Ryu and Ander kill grappling minions while Wazir's shatter and homunculus attacks help thin the field, and several lesser creatures start fleeing for the water. The boss reappears outside the chamber, lashes out at Ander and Trok, and even kills one of its own fleeing servants for cowardice, but Trok redirects one of its whip strikes and Ryu marks it before a trident blow makes the elemental collapse into a puddle. In the next few minutes, the party kills the last two creatures before they can swim off and threaten Rayna.

With combat over, the party gathers around the crystal and Nerissa hears a faint melody of peaceful sleep and quiet gratitude. A quick search confirms that the shrine is mostly ruined and flooded, with little left beyond the offerings Trok already looted, so the group leaves the cave and swims back to Rayna. When they hand her the fragment, everyone shares a vision of an undersea home being rebuilt among kelp and fishfolk; Rayna recognizes the destination and says the crystal can guide her. The party returns to the Leviathan Inn that afternoon, helps Rayna settle in for one more night, and learns she means to sail across the Mawar Ocean toward Apporia and the merfolk city of Omni in the morning.

sourceEntries.title: Finding the Buried Shrine
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The next phase is the next day's search itself: the party takes Ryu's boat to the North Cliff, uses scouting and local memory to find the right collapsed sea cave, and confirms they have reached the buried shrine of Guzo.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The next morning the party sails to the North Cliff, finds the old collapse, and confirms that a blasted opening now leads back into the buried shrine cave.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After sleeping on the plan, the group meets the next morning, takes breakfast, and boards Ryu's small boat with Rayna along for the ride. They follow the North Cliff shoreline to the place Ovina described, use scouting and shared checks to study the rockfall, and realize that the cave has recently been punched open again by some force far cleaner than a natural collapse. With Ryu linked through Turk's senses and Wazir's mechanical fish scouting alongside, they confirm there is still a navigable way into the old shrine beyond the rubble.

By the end of the exchange, the party settles on bottling the creature up inside the shrine and killing it rather than trusting any bargain.

recapBlocks.title: Finding the Buried Shrine
locationRefs: North Cliff sea cave outside Hamri
sourceShortSummaries: The next morning the party sails to the North Cliff, finds the old collapse, and confirms that a blasted opening now leads back into the buried shrine cave.
sourceLongSummaries: After sleeping on the plan, the group meets the next morning, takes breakfast, and boards Ryu's small boat with Rayna along for the ride. They follow the North Cliff shoreline to the place Ovina described, use scouting and shared checks to study the rockfall, and realize that the cave has recently been punched open again by some force far cleaner than a natural collapse. With Ryu linked through Turk's senses and Wazir's mechanical fish scouting alongside, they confirm there is still a navigable way into the old shrine beyond the rubble.

sourceEntries.title: Finding the Buried Shrine
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The next phase is the next day's search itself: the party takes Ryu's boat to the North Cliff, uses scouting and local memory to find the right collapsed sea cave, and confirms they have reached the buried shrine of Guzo.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The next morning the party sails to the North Cliff, finds the old collapse, and confirms that a blasted opening now leads back into the buried shrine cave.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After sleeping on the plan, the group meets the next morning, takes breakfast, and boards Ryu's small boat with Rayna along for the ride. They follow the North Cliff shoreline to the place Ovina described, use scouting and shared checks to study the rockfall, and realize that the cave has recently been punched open again by some force far cleaner than a natural collapse. With Ryu linked through Turk's senses and Wazir's mechanical fish scouting alongside, they confirm there is still a navigable way into the old shrine beyond the rubble.

refs: North Cliff sea cave outside Hamri
locationHints.context: morning boat trip and shoreline search for the collapsed Guzo shrine
locationHints.notes: The beat ends once the party identifies the blasted passage back into the cave.

npcDetails.name: Rayna
npcDetails.context: sailor accompanying the search by boat
npcDetails.notes: She joins the ride out and helps confirm that the party has reached the place from her vision.

recapBlocks.title: Into the Sea Cave
locationRefs: buried sea cave shrine of Guzo
sourceShortSummaries: Swimming through the breach, the party finds the blue-lit beach and ruined outer temple of Guzo's shrine, spots idle water creatures, and advances toward the building.
sourceLongSummaries: The party dives through the newly blasted underwater opening and surfaces inside a hidden sea cave whose interior matches Rayna's vision: a warm blue glow, a strip of sandy beach, and a white shrine building on the shore. They quickly realize the collapse was not the only recent disturbance, because the breach and the shattered doorway deeper inside both look as if magic blasted straight through stone and timber. A cluster of watery, frog-like shrine creatures loiters around the beach and temple, and after sizing up the scene the party presses inward instead of retreating for a longer plan.

sourceEntries.title: Into the Sea Cave
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Once they dive through the blasted opening, the whole beat is about first contact inside the cave: scouting the beach, spotting the water creatures, approaching the ruined temple, and browbeating the trapped minions into cooperating and leading them inward.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Swimming through the breach, the party finds the blue-lit beach and ruined outer temple of Guzo's shrine, spots idle water creatures, and advances toward the building.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party dives through the newly blasted underwater opening and surfaces inside a hidden sea cave whose interior matches Rayna's vision: a warm blue glow, a strip of sandy beach, and a white shrine building on the shore. They quickly realize the collapse was not the only recent disturbance, because the breach and the shattered doorway deeper inside both look as if magic blasted straight through stone and timber. A cluster of watery, frog-like shrine creatures loiters around the beach and temple, and after sizing up the scene the party presses inward instead of retreating for a longer plan.

refs: buried sea cave shrine of Guzo
locationHints.context: sandy inner beach and ruined outer temple beyond the blasted underwater passage
locationHints.notes: This is the first direct entry into the hidden shrine cave Rayna saw in her vision.

itemDetails.name: shattered reinforced door
itemDetails.notes: Broken chunks of the inner doorway show that something recently blasted deeper into the shrine.

recapBlocks.title: Secrets of Guzo's Shrine
sourceShortSummaries: Inside the shrine, the party uncovers books and offerings, finds the ancestor prayer that opens a hidden stone passage, and reveals a trapped water elemental clutching the crystal fragment.
sourceLongSummaries: With the outer approach secured for the moment, the party searches the main temple and the side chapel in earnest. They study the statue of Guzo, the glowing altar, scattered offerings, and a damaged stone chest that still preserves a prayer book, a saintly story collection, and a ledger of prayers left at the shrine. The Mawarin inscription about kneeling and praying Guzo's ancestor prayer leads Nerissa to the right text, and when she sings it the hidden slab door vanishes to expose a deeper chamber. Inside waits a large water elemental figure pinned by the shrine's blue light and holding the crystal fragment the party came to recover.

sourceEntries.title: Secrets of Guzo's Shrine
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Inside the temple the party investigates the statue, inscriptions, offerings, and chapel, discovers the prayer that opens the hidden passage, and reveals the trapped water boss holding the song fragment behind the vanished stone door.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Inside the shrine, the party uncovers books and offerings, finds the ancestor prayer that opens a hidden stone passage, and reveals a trapped water elemental clutching the crystal fragment.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With the outer approach secured for the moment, the party searches the main temple and the side chapel in earnest. They study the statue of Guzo, the glowing altar, scattered offerings, and a damaged stone chest that still preserves a prayer book, a saintly story collection, and a ledger of prayers left at the shrine. The Mawarin inscription about kneeling and praying Guzo's ancestor prayer leads Nerissa to the right text, and when she sings it the hidden slab door vanishes to expose a deeper chamber. Inside waits a large water elemental figure pinned by the shrine's blue light and holding the crystal fragment the party came to recover.

locationHints.context: main sanctuary, side chapel, and newly revealed hidden chamber
locationHints.notes: The beat tracks the party from temple investigation to the revelation of the trapped inner-chamber intruder.

npcDetails.name: Guzo the Mariner
npcDetails.context: saint honored by the shrine's altar, prayer, and inscriptions
npcDetails.notes: The hidden passage opens only through Guzo's ancestor prayer.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: power invoked by the trapped elemental's minion
npcDetails.notes: The elemental's servant blurts out that the crystal is meant for Gazankoa.

itemDetails.name: prayer book
itemDetails.notes: A preserved book in the chapel chest contains Guzo's ancestor prayer.

itemDetails.name: saints' story book
itemDetails.notes: An illuminated collection of local saint stories survives in the same stone chest.

itemDetails.name: prayer ledger
itemDetails.notes: A damaged guestbook preserves prayers and hopes once left at the shrine.

itemDetails.name: song fragment crystal
itemDetails.notes: The hidden chamber contains the crystal fragment the ocean wants restored.

recapBlocks.title: A Bad Deal Offered
sourceShortSummaries: The trapped elemental offers to spare the party if they free it and let it take the crystal to Gazankoa, but the blue light's binding effect and the creature's threats convince the group to fight instead.
sourceLongSummaries: Before steel is drawn, the party talks with the elemental in the hidden chamber and learns how badly things have gone inside the shrine. The creature explains that it forced its way in, seized the crystal fragment, and triggered the blue light bound to the altar, which now prevents its teleportation and keeps it trapped despite the opened passage. It offers to return to the plane of water and even let the party present the crystal to Gazankoa, but the more it talks the clearer it becomes that it will simply keep hunting the shard and anyone carrying it. By the end of the exchange, the party settles on bottling the creature up inside the shrine and killing it rather than trusting any bargain.

sourceEntries.title: A Bad Deal Offered
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Before initiative starts, the party tries to bluff and bargain with the trapped elemental, learns how the blue light and shard confinement work, debates whether to let him go, and finally shifts from talk to a plan to bottle him up and fight in the temple.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The trapped elemental offers to spare the party if they free it and let it take the crystal to Gazankoa, but the blue light's binding effect and the creature's threats convince the group to fight instead.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Before steel is drawn, the party talks with the elemental in the hidden chamber and learns how badly things have gone inside the shrine. The creature explains that it forced its way in, seized the crystal fragment, and triggered the blue light bound to the altar, which now prevents its teleportation and keeps it trapped despite the opened passage. It offers to return to the plane of water and even let the party present the crystal to Gazankoa, but the more it talks the clearer it becomes that it will simply keep hunting the shard and anyone carrying it. By the end of the exchange, the party settles on bottling the creature up inside the shrine and killing it rather than trusting any bargain.

locationHints.context: threshold between the glowing altar room and the elemental's prison chamber
locationHints.notes: The whole beat is a tense negotiation at the doorway before initiative begins.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: the power the elemental claims to serve
npcDetails.notes: The elemental frames the crystal as something that should be brought to Gazankoa.

itemDetails.name: song fragment crystal
itemDetails.notes: The crystal remains in the elemental's grasp during the negotiation and drives the whole argument.

recapBlocks.title: Jet of Water to Mist and Last Blows
locationRefs: Guzo shrine inner temple and sea cave beach
sourceShortSummaries: The elemental opens the fight with a brutal jet of water, hurling the party back, dropping Geo, and turning the doorway ambush into a hard scramble for control.
The shrine's blue light begins to work against the elemental, and Nerissa's magic drives it back while the lesser creatures crowd the stairs and start grappling the front line.
The wounded elemental turns to mist, slips through the wall, punishes fleeing minions, and is finally destroyed before the party cuts down the last shrine creatures.
sourceLongSummaries: When the parley breaks down, initiative starts and the trapped elemental wastes no time, lashing the shrine interior with a massive jet of frigid water. The opening blast batters Trok, Ryu, Ander, Geo, and the nearby shrine creatures, immediately showing how dangerous the foe is even while confined by the blue light. Ryu recovers enough to answer with thrown trident strikes and Hunter's Mark, Ander fights his way back into the doorway, and the party's neat plan to contain the creature turns into a messy opening brawl in the temple.

As the melee settles into its middle phase, the same blue glow binding the elemental starts actively helping the party. Nerissa's dissonant whispers bite especially hard under the altar's influence and force the elemental backward into the chamber, where opportunity attacks from Trok and Wazir leave it staggering and badly wounded. The lesser shrine creatures rush the stairs and doorway, hardening their bodies and then grappling Ryu and Ander with watery scimitars, but the party keeps pressure on the main foe until it is below a quarter of its strength and ready to change tactics.

The final phase begins when the badly wounded elemental abandons a straight fight and turns into watery mist to move away from the party. Ryu and Ander kill grappling minions while Wazir's shatter and homunculus attacks help thin the field, and several lesser creatures start fleeing for the water. The boss reappears outside the chamber, lashes out at Ander and Trok, and even kills one of its own fleeing servants for cowardice, but Trok redirects one of its whip strikes and Ryu marks it before a trident blow makes the elemental collapse into a puddle. In the next few minutes, the party kills the last two creatures before they can swim off and threaten Rayna.

sourceEntries.title: Jet of Water
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Initiative begins with the party's attempted bottleneck plan turning into open combat: the boss lashes out with the first massive water jet, the front line scrambles to contain the doorway, and the opening exchange ends with Geo dropping and the boss still entrenched.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The elemental opens the fight with a brutal jet of water, hurling the party back, dropping Geo, and turning the doorway ambush into a hard scramble for control.
sourceEntries.longSummary: When the parley breaks down, initiative starts and the trapped elemental wastes no time, lashing the shrine interior with a massive jet of frigid water. The opening blast batters Trok, Ryu, Ander, Geo, and the nearby shrine creatures, immediately showing how dangerous the foe is even while confined by the blue light. Ryu recovers enough to answer with thrown trident strikes and Hunter's Mark, Ander fights his way back into the doorway, and the party's neat plan to contain the creature turns into a messy opening brawl in the temple.

locationHints.context: altar room and doorway to the hidden chamber during the opening combat exchange

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The crystal-holding intruder trapped by the shrine's blue light opens the fight with overwhelming force.

mainEnemies.name: shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The lesser watery creatures around the shrine support the elemental during the first rounds.

combatHints.notes: The opening rounds are defined by the elemental's water jet, the party's disrupted formation, and the sudden realization that the fight will not be quick.

sourceEntries.title: Blue Light Advantage
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The middle of the fight turns in the party's favor: Nerissa's spell and the shrine's blue glow drive the boss back, the minions flood the stairs, and focused blows leave the trapped elemental badly wounded before it changes tactics.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The shrine's blue light begins to work against the elemental, and Nerissa's magic drives it back while the lesser creatures crowd the stairs and start grappling the front line.
sourceEntries.longSummary: As the melee settles into its middle phase, the same blue glow binding the elemental starts actively helping the party. Nerissa's dissonant whispers bite especially hard under the altar's influence and force the elemental backward into the chamber, where opportunity attacks from Trok and Wazir leave it staggering and badly wounded. The lesser shrine creatures rush the stairs and doorway, hardening their bodies and then grappling Ryu and Ander with watery scimitars, but the party keeps pressure on the main foe until it is below a quarter of its strength and ready to change tactics.

locationHints.context: altar platform, doorway, and stair choke point as the party drives the elemental back and the minions press forward
locationHints.notes: The blue-lit sanctuary itself becomes part of the party's advantage during this phase.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The main foe is bloodied, then pushed below a quarter of its strength, while visibly hindered by the shrine's blue light.

mainEnemies.name: shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The lesser creatures surge forward, harden defensively, and begin grappling Ryu and Ander at the stairs.

combatHints.notes: The middle turns when the shrine's magic and Nerissa's spell combine to drive the elemental back, even as its minions pin down the front line.

sourceEntries.title: Mist and Last Blows
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: With the boss desperate, the battle shifts into its final phase: the elemental turns to mist, slips through the wall, punishes fleeing minions, and is finally destroyed before the party finishes the last creatures.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The wounded elemental turns to mist, slips through the wall, punishes fleeing minions, and is finally destroyed before the party cuts down the last shrine creatures.

Ryu and Ander kill grappling minions while Wazir's shatter and homunculus attacks help thin the field, and several lesser creatures start fleeing for the water. The boss reappears outside the chamber, lashes out at Ander and Trok, and even kills one of its own fleeing servants for cowardice, but Trok redirects one of its whip strikes and Ryu marks it before a trident blow makes the elemental collapse into a puddle. In the next few minutes, the party kills the last two creatures before they can swim off and threaten Rayna.

mainEnemies.name: remaining shrine water creatures

combatHints.notes: The combat ends once Ryu's marked trident strike collapses the elemental and the party finishes the final two creatures.

recapBlocks.title: The Fragment Returns
sourceShortSummaries: After the fight, Nerissa hears the recovered crystal's grateful song, the party returns it to Rayna, and Rayna accepts the task of carrying it to Omni.
sourceLongSummaries: With combat over, the party gathers around the crystal and Nerissa hears a faint melody of peaceful sleep and quiet gratitude. A quick search confirms that the shrine is mostly ruined and flooded, with little left beyond the offerings Trok already looted, so the group leaves the cave and swims back to Rayna. When they hand her the fragment, everyone shares a vision of an undersea home being rebuilt among kelp and fishfolk; Rayna recognizes the destination and says the crystal can guide her. The party returns to the Leviathan Inn that afternoon, helps Rayna settle in for one more night, and learns she means to sail across the Mawar Ocean toward Apporia and the merfolk city of Omni in the morning.

sourceEntries.title: The Fragment Returns

A quick search confirms that the shrine is mostly ruined and flooded, with little left beyond the offerings Trok already looted, so the group leaves the cave and swims back to Rayna. When they hand her the fragment, everyone shares a vision of an undersea home being rebuilt among kelp and fishfolk; Rayna recognizes the destination and says the crystal can guide her. The party returns to the Leviathan Inn that afternoon, helps Rayna settle in for one more night, and learns she means to sail across the Mawar Ocean toward Apporia and the merfolk city of Omni in the morning.
